title: Discover Canada - Memorable Quotes - Canada.ca

## Discover Canada - Memorable Quotes
Discover Canada: The Rights and Responsibilities of Citizenship - Memorable Quotes
Duration: 1 minute, 07 seconds. Read by Albert Schultz .
Download this chapter: MP3 ( MP3 , 1.03 MB )
You can also download all of Discover Canada ( MP3 , 155.94 MB ) as a single file.
The audio may take a moment to load.
“For here [in Canada], I want the marble to remain the marble; the granite to remain the granite; the oak to remain the oak; and out of these elements, I would build a nation great among the nations of the world.”
— Sir Wilfrid Laurier 7 th Prime Minister of Canada July 11, 1896 – October 6, 1911
“I am a Canadian, a free Canadian, free to speak without fear, free to worship in my own way, free to stand for what I think right, free to oppose what I believe wrong, or free to choose those who shall govern my country. This heritage of freedom I pledge to uphold for myself and all mankind.”
— John Diefenbaker 13 th Prime Minister of Canada June 21, 1957 – April 22, 1963
These quotes do not need to be learned for the citizenship test.
Team Canada won gold in men’s hockey at the 2010 Winter Olympics in Vancouver
